steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks which choice "provides an example of a SNOTEL site." An example means naming or identifying a specific, concrete instance — not describing what SNOTEL sites do or who maintains them.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'The data collected by SNOTEL sites can help researchers predict downstream water supplies.': Describes what the data does, not an example of a SNOTEL site. No specific site is named. ✗
  • Choice B — 'The SNOTEL site at Bear Trap Meadow is one of around 90 automated snow measurement stations in Wyoming.': Names a specific SNOTEL site (Bear Trap Meadow) and places it in context — this is exactly an example of a SNOTEL site. ✓
  • Choice C — 'Snow hydrologist Julie Koeberle helps maintain automated snow monitoring stations known as SNOTEL sites.': Defines what SNOTEL sites are and mentions who maintains them. No specific site is named. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Located throughout Wyoming, SNOTEL sites monitor snow conditions.': States where they are generally located and what they do. Still no specific site. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
